name: AI Fundamentals for Modern Developers
description: A practical foundation in AI and machine learning concepts tailored for software engineers. Covers core theory, modern LLMs, embeddings, vector search, and responsible AI deployment — all through a hands-on engineering lens.
targetAudience: Software engineers with no prior ML background who want to understand and work with AI systems

steps.title: AI & ML Landscape: What Every Engineer Must Know
steps.description: Understand the current AI ecosystem: the difference between classical ML, deep learning, foundation models, and generative AI. Map these to real engineering use cases.

substeps.title: Read: The three waves of AI and where we are now
Identify: How AI is used in 5 systems you already use
Decide: Which AI paradigm is relevant to your team's product

steps.title: Language Models: How LLMs Actually Work
steps.description: Demystify transformer architecture, tokenization, attention mechanisms, and training at a conceptual level. Understand context windows, temperature, and sampling strategies.

substeps.title: Study: Transformer architecture in plain English
Experiment: Explore tokenization with tiktoken or similar
Experiment: Observe the effect of temperature on outputs

steps.title: Embeddings & Semantic Search
steps.description: Understand vector embeddings and how they enable semantic similarity, classification, and retrieval-augmented generation (RAG). Build a minimal semantic search system.

substeps.title: Learn: What embeddings are and how they encode meaning
Build: Generate embeddings for a small document set
Build: Semantic search with cosine similarity
Verify: Compare semantic vs keyword search quality

steps.title: Vector Databases: Scaling Semantic Search
steps.description: Explore purpose-built vector databases (Pinecone, Weaviate, Chroma, pgvector). Understand indexing strategies, ANN search, and when to use each option.

substeps.title: Compare: Pinecone vs Weaviate vs Chroma vs pgvector
Build: Index 1,000 documents in a local vector DB
Benchmark: Query latency at different scales

steps.title: Model Evaluation & Benchmarking
steps.description: Learn how to measure AI model quality. Understand evaluation metrics (accuracy, BLEU, ROUGE, LLM-as-judge) and how to run repeatable evals for your use case.

substeps.title: Study: Evaluation frameworks and what they measure
Build: A simple eval harness for an LLM task
Verify: Run your eval against two different models

steps.title: Responsible AI: Bias, Fairness, and Production Safety
steps.description: Understand how bias enters AI systems, how to detect and mitigate it, content safety considerations, and how to build guardrails for production deployments.

substeps.title: Study: Sources of bias in AI training data and models
Audit: Run a bias analysis on a public dataset
Design: Content safety guardrails for your team's use case
Verify: Review output against fairness checklist
